subject: Cloud Computing
title: Heroku: The PaaS Pioneer
description: A micro-lesson that makes Heroku: The PaaS Pioneer usable.

hook.text: In 2007, three developers built a platform where you could deploy a web app with one command.

buildup.text: Heroku handled scaling, databases, and infrastructure. Developers just wrote code and pushed it.

discovery.text: Salesforce bought Heroku for $212 million in 2010, validating PaaS as a real business model.

twist.text: Heroku's simplicity came at a cost: limited customization and prices that climbed fast at scale.

climax.text: Heroku inspired a generation of platforms: Vercel, Render, Railway. The PaaS idea spread everywhere.

punchline.text: Heroku showed developers what 'just works' could mean.

quiz.question: What was Heroku's key innovation?
options: Letting developers deploy web apps with minimal infrastructure knowledge
Creating the first cloud storage service
